channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Beneath King Charles’s Jokes and Decorum, Some Subtle Rebuttals to Trump
item.description: King Charles III gently pushed back against President Trump’s attacks on Britain and NATO, and he spoke of the importance of checks and balances.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: King Charles III addressing Congress on Tuesday.

item.title: At State Dinner, King Charles Charms the Court of Trump
item.description: King Charles III presented President Trump with a golden bell. “Should you ever need to get hold of us,” the king said, “well, just give us a ring!”
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: King Charles III at the state dinner at the White House on Tuesday night, delivering a speech with the right ingredients for President Trump's second term.

item.title: See the Menu for the British Royal State Dinner
item.description: Spring-herbed ravioli and Dover sole meunière are among the dishes at the dinner honoring King Charles III of Britain and Queen Camilla.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: A dinner menu for the evening, seen at the White House.

item.title: Full Guest List for Trump’s State Dinner With Charles and Camilla
item.description: Six members of the Supreme Court were invited to the dinner, alongside top administration officials, billionaires and Republican lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: President Trump and the first lady, Melania Trump, welcomed King Charles III of Britain and Queen Camilla to the White House on Tuesday evening for a state dinner.

item.title: DOJ Secures New James Comey Indictment Over Alleged Threat Against Trump
item.description: The new case stems from a social media post showing seashells on a North Carolina beach that the Trump administration characterized as a threat against the president.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett and Tyler Pager
item.media:credit: Monica Jorge for The New York Times
item.media:description: James B. Comey, the former F.B.I. director, was indicted last year by a grand jury in Virginia, accused of lying and obstructing a congressional investigation over testimony he gave in 2020. The charge was later dismissed.

item.title: FCC Orders a Review of ABC’s Licenses Amid Feud Between Trump and Kimmel
item.description: The agency said the review was related to the network’s diversity and inclusion policies. But it came amid a fight between the president and the network’s late night host, Jimmy Kimmel.
item.dc:creator: Jim Rutenberg and John Koblin
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Brendan Carr, the chairman of the Federal Communications Commission, has repeatedly threatened to take action against broadcasters.

item.title: What to Know About the Kimmel vs. Trump Feud
item.description: The president and first lady took exception to a joke. Jimmy Kimmel defended it.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jimmy Kimmel said of a line that annoyed the president: “It obviously was a joke.”

item.title: OpenAI Trial Starts With Two Very Different Tales of a Company’s Early Years
item.description: In the trial’s first day of testimony, Elon Musk said greed led co-founder Sam Altman to pull the A.I. lab away from its nonprofit roots. OpenAI says that’s nonsense.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Brennan Smart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk arriving at the Ronald V. Dellums U.S. Courthouse in Oakland Tuesday.

item.title: Amid Iran War and Tensions with Neighbors, U.A.E. Goes Its Own Way
item.description: The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC has rocked the region, underscoring how the country, at odds with Saudi Arabia, is increasingly charting its own course.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim and Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
item.media:description: Smoke billowing from an oil facility in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, in March.

item.title: Britain Summons Iran’s Ambassador Over Message to Iranians in U.K.
item.description: Iran’s embassy in London posted a message on social media inviting Iranians living in Britain to register for a ‘Sacrifice for the Homeland’ campaign.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: The Iranian ambassador to Britain, Seyed Ali Mousavi, presenting his credentials to King Charles III at Buckingham Palace in June.

item.title: Gallows Humor Provides Respite From Mideast Wars
item.description: Arab digital creators on social media are turning out edgy material to cope with the conflicts and bring levity to a pain that often feels too raw.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Samer Moumneh, wearing a red headband, recording a new sketch in a cafe in Beirut, Lebanon, last week.

item.title: Trump’s Face Will Appear on Limited-Edition U.S. Passports, State Dept. Says
item.description: The department said it planned to release “a limited number of specially designed” U.S. passports that feature a picture of the president to commemorate the country’s 250th anniversary in July.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson
item.media:description: An image of the proposed limited-edition passport design released by the State Department.

item.title: Former Fauci Adviser Indicted on Covid-Related Charges
item.description: Prosecutors accused Dr. David Morens, a former adviser to Dr. Anthony S. Fauci, of hiding records related to the onset of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mueller
item.media:description: Dr. David Morens, a former senior adviser to Dr. Anthony S. Fauci at the National Institutes of Health, in 2024 during an appearance before a House committee.

item.title: White House Urges House to Quickly Fund D.H.S.
item.description: The call amounted to a rebuke of Speaker Mike Johnson, who has delayed action on a homeland security spending bill and suggested this week that it needed changes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: The memo calls for the “immediate passage” of a stalled bill to reopen the Department of Homeland Security.

item.title: Kennedy Center Works to Make the Case That Repairs Are Urgent
item.description: In Washington and in federal court, the center is arguing that its planned two-year closure is crucial. Critics say it’s a result of declining attendance and fleeing artists.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs and Zach Montague
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Among the changes the Trump administration has brought to the Kennedy Center is the addition of the president’s name to the exterior. Now it is slated to close for reasons that are the subject of debate.

item.title: Global Deforestation Slows, W.R.I. Report Finds. But Wildfires Are Taking a Toll.
item.description: In 2025, the world razed less forest than any other year in the last decade. The bad news: global warming is making wildfires more frequent and intense.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey and Harry Stevens

item.title: Trump Tests the Limits of His Most Faithful Supporters
item.description: The president’s rhetoric on religion, along with hard-line immigration policies and the U.S. war in Iran, has splintered a coalition of Christian voters who returned him to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump at the National Prayer Breakfast in February.

item.title: Takeaways From the California Governor Debate at Pomona College
item.description: The candidates felt pressure to differentiate themselves, with ballots going out to voters next week. The result was a more spirited battle than past debates.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Soumya Karlamangla
item.media:description: Eight candidates took the stage at Pomona College in one of the final debates in the California gubernatorial primary race.

item.title: The War in Iran Has Americans Rethinking Their Summer Travel Plans
item.description: Faced with geopolitical instability, economic uncertainty and rising airfares, many travelers are changing destinations or canceling their trips entirely.
item.dc:creator: Ceylan Yeğinsu
item.media:description: Data from the aviation analytics firm Cirium shows a 10.5 percent decline in July bookings from the U.S. to Europe compared with last year.

item.title: Could At-Home Brain Stimulation Reduce Psychiatry’s Reliance on S.S.R.I.s?
item.description: A headset recently approved by the Food and Drug Administration uses a weak electric current to shock the brain. Some researchers hope it could challenge the current pill-centric paradigm.
item.dc:creator: Rachel E. Gross
item.media:credit: Joshua Bright for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sophie Davies, wearing her transcranial direct-current stimulation headset at home in Suffolk, England, to manage her depression and obsessive-compulsive disorder.

item.title: Supreme Court Appears Skeptical of Falun Gong Lawsuit Against Cisco
item.description: The court’s decision could have broader implications for lawsuits seeking to hold companies liable for international human rights abuses.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Jonah Markowitz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of Falun Gong during a protest meditation in Manhattan in 2024. The Chinese government has banned the group.

item.title: Offbeat Obituaries Celebrate Loss With Levity (and Brutal Honesty)
item.description: Irreverent tributes filled with unvarnished truths and funny anecdotes, which run counter to more somber traditional obituaries, have gained attention for “how alive they feel,” a researcher said.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

item.title: The Banality of Evil, Again
item.description: The distinguishing feature of Cole Tomas Allen’s manifesto is its insipidity.
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens

item.title: I Played Putin in a War Game. He Could Invade Again.
item.description: NATO countries need to get their act together.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Gabuev

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Spills the Tea on Trump’s Royal Meeting
item.description: The late night show host called the king of England “one of the very few people who might actually be able to tip things even slightly in a positive direction.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Dirk Kempthorne, Former Idaho Governor and Interior Secretary, Dies at 74
item.description: A Republican, Mr. Kempthorne rose to prominence as the mayor of Boise in the late 1980s and early ’90s, as the city became a haven for outdoor enthusiasts.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dirk Kempthorne testifying in 2008. Before his tenure as interior secretary, he was elected as the governor of Idaho in 1998 and remained in office until 2006.

item.title: Oakland’s Airport Can Use ‘San Francisco’ in Its Name Under Settlement
item.description: The agreement ends a two-year dispute between the Bay Area cities over the smaller airport’s rebranding.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:description: San Francisco had sued over the Oakland airport name in 2024.

item.title: Mamdani’s Candidate Concedes in High-Profile Council Race in Manhattan
item.description: The City Council special election pitting Lindsey Boylan, backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani, against Carl Wilson, a community activist, was not officially called. But Ms. Boylan conceded.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack
item.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
item.media:description: tk

item.title: ‘Dances With Wolves’ Actor Is Sentenced to Life in Prison
item.description: Nathan Chasing Horse had been convicted of charges including the sexual assault of women and girls and the possession of child sexual abuse imagery.
item.dc:creator: Reggie Ugwu
item.media:description: Nathan Chasing Horse, in court in January. He had pleaded not guilty to all charges against him.
